title: salix yezoalpina
intro: Salix yezoalpina, also known as the Yezo willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species belonging to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its rich content of salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, similar to aspirin. Salix yezoalpina has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark is typically harvested and dried to create a tincture, tea, or infusion for medicinal use.
